writer_persona: a showrunner for character-driven, relationship-first serial drama in the vein of prestige ensemble tech-thrillers — analog dread meets corporate menace, always filtered through who trusts whom
style_guidelines: Every scene serves character relationships first, plot second — investigation details land through arguments, confessions, and misplaced trust, not exposition dumps.
Dialogue is naturalistic and interrupted; characters talk over each other, deflect, and change subject when a topic gets too close to their own secret.
Escalate dread through small sensory details (a still-warm stove, a crayon symbol, a signal spike on a monitor) rather than jump scares or gore.
No character ever says more than their own knowledge slice allows — a confidant may know what a lover doesn't, and the audience should be ahead of at least one character in every scene.
Keep procedural/technical jargon light and translated into plain stakes a listener can feel, never a manual.

NARRATOR.default_style: calm, measured, a step removed from the action
NARRATOR.description: the show's narrator — orients the listener at the start of every scene (who, where, when), never a character in the story

JONAH_REESE.default_style: steady, clipped, the voice of someone who has trained himself not to sound as worried as he is
JONAH_REESE.description: lead investigator for the digital forensics unit; native English speaker; still carries guilt from a prior case where he dismissed a whistleblower who turned out to be right, and is determined not to make that mistake twice.

DR_MEI_LIN.default_style: gentle, precise, chooses words carefully in her second language — a trained clinician's calm, speaks slow and deliberate English
DR_MEI_LIN.description: child trauma psychologist attached to the investigation to work with Milo, the sole survivor; emigrated from Chengdu as a teenager and has built her career reading what silence hides — she believes she's protecting the last witness, blind to what he's really protecting.

WEI_ZHOU.default_style: quick, technical, occasionally slips into rapid-fire jargon before catching himself and slowing down for the room
WEI_ZHOU.description: signals and audio forensics analyst, Beijing-born, moved to the US for graduate work in acoustic engineering; his cousin's family lived in Amity Hollow, so this case is personal in a way he hasn't told the team.

YUNA_CHO.default_style: smooth, professionally warm, every answer sounds rehearsed even when it's true
YUNA_CHO.description: government liaison assigned to facilitate the team's access to the restricted zone; Korean-American, raised between Seoul and the Pacific Northwest — officially there to help; unofficially reporting every step of the investigation to someone else.

OLD_CHEN.default_style: gravelly, unhurried, a storyteller's cadence that circles a subject before landing on it
OLD_CHEN.description: retired systems engineer living off-grid near the ridge; decades ago helped build the original underground facility before it was taken over and repurposed; carries a guilt he's never spoken aloud.

MILO.default_style: rarely speaks above a whisper; when he does, each word is careful, flattened by years in a household that mixed three languages
MILO.description: the sole survivor, a boy of about ten found alone in the village clutching a drawing of a recurring symbol; refuses to speak to anyone but Dr. Lin — or so it appears.

AMITY_HOLLOW_SQUARE.name: Amity Hollow Square
AMITY_HOLLOW_SQUARE.description: the vanished village's central square, frozen mid-evening — warm string lights still flickering over empty porches and tables set with half-eaten meals gone cold.

THE_LAST_HOUSE.name: The Last House
THE_LAST_HOUSE.description: the specific cottage where Milo was found, its walls covered floor to ceiling in a child's crayon renderings of the same cryptic symbol, over and over.

MOBILE_FORENSICS_UNIT.name: Mobile Forensics Unit
MOBILE_FORENSICS_UNIT.description: the team's converted trailer parked at the village edge, banks of monitors and evidence bags glowing blue-white against the dark treeline outside.

FIELD_OPS_TENT.name: Field Ops Tent
FIELD_OPS_TENT.description: a canvas command tent at the village perimeter, maps pinned with red string and radios crackling under a single swinging work light.

THE_RIDGE_TRAILHEAD.name: The Ridge Trailhead
THE_RIDGE_TRAILHEAD.description: the forest edge where every resident's last phone ping stops, muddy boot prints trailing into the trees and simply ending.

HALCYON_VANTAGE_LOBBY.name: Halcyon Vantage Lobby
HALCYON_VANTAGE_LOBBY.description: the corporation's regional headquarters lobby, all glass and brushed steel, PR-polished and watched by cameras that never blink.

RESTRICTED_ZONE_CHECKPOINT.name: Restricted Zone Checkpoint
RESTRICTED_ZONE_CHECKPOINT.description: a military gate at the edge of government land, floodlit and fenced in razor wire, armed guards who ask no questions and answer none.

THE_BUNKER_ATRIUM.name: The Bunker Atrium
THE_BUNKER_ATRIUM.description: a vast underground chamber beneath the mountain, brutalist concrete and rows of dormant screens, unnervingly clean for a place built to disappear.

THE_BUNKER_OBSERVATION_DECK.name: The Bunker Observation Deck
THE_BUNKER_OBSERVATION_DECK.description: one-way glass overlooking rows of sealed chambers below, cold blue instrument light reflected in a bank of silent control panels.

OLD_CHEN_CABIN.name: Old Chen's Cabin
OLD_CHEN_CABIN.description: a hand-built off-grid cabin cluttered with yellowed blueprints and a ham radio rig, wood smoke and kerosene in the air.

TRAUMA_SAFE_ROOM.name: Trauma Safe Room
TRAUMA_SAFE_ROOM.description: a quiet clinical room at the base camp where Mei Lin works with Milo — soft lamplight, drawing supplies, and a one-way mirror no one mentions.

THE_BROADCAST_VAN.name: The Broadcast Van
THE_BROADCAST_VAN.description: a hijacked satellite uplink van, cramped and wire-tangled, its dashboard lights the only color in a night lit by nothing else.

audio.default_style: Speak clearly and with dramatic weight

series_intro: Every device in the mountain village of Amity Hollow is still humming when the digital forensics team arrives — hot stoves, half-eaten dinners, phones charging on nightstands — but every one of its forty-one residents is gone without a single struggle. The only living witness is a ten-year-old boy who won't speak, and the only clue is a symbol he keeps drawing, one that turns up hidden inside the village's own security footage. This season follows the investigators sent to find out where the residents went — and the secrets, rivalries, and quiet betrayals inside their own team that complicate every step closer to the truth.
series_outro: The world sees the raw footage — the signal, the lured march into the trees, the bunker beneath the restricted zone — and Halcyon Vantage's name becomes a scandal overnight. Yuna Cho's double life is exposed and she is taken into custody; Old Chen finally says aloud what he built and who he lost. But the investigators' triumph curdles in the final frame: the boy they carried out of Amity Hollow, the one they thought they saved, was never the last piece of evidence — he was the first plant of the next one, and nobody yet knows how many other quiet, unspeaking children are already waiting in villages no one has found yet.

episodes.theme: The forensics team arrives at the emptied village of Amity Hollow, finds Milo alone and silent, and opens the first encrypted devices — while introducing the team's own fault lines: Jonah's need to prove himself, Yuna's suspiciously smooth access to the restricted zone, and Mei Lin's first attempt to reach the boy who won't speak.
episodes.outro_note: As night settles over the Mobile Forensics Unit, the first corrupted camera file finally finishes rendering — and buried in the static, unmistakable, is the outline of the same symbol Milo has been drawing on every wall of the Last House.

case.label: The Vanishing Signal
case.solution: Halcyon Vantage covertly modified every smart-home hub in Amity Hollow with an unlogged firmware push that emitted an inaudible sub-20Hz compliance signal, encoded with a steganographic watermark matching Milo's crayon symbol; the signal walked all forty-one residents out of their homes and into the woods to a concealed entrance of the corporation's underground bunker network, where they were taken in for a mass behavioral-conditioning experiment. Milo was not the accident's lone survivor — he was placed in the village beforehand and left behind afterward, conditioned to feign muteness and trauma and to keep drawing the symbol so that any investigation would be guided, step by step, straight to the bunker.
objective_facts: All forty-one residents' last phone pings hit the same relay tower at 9:47 p.m., moments before every signal cut out at the ridge treeline.
Every smart-home hub in Amity Hollow received an unlogged firmware push roughly six hours before the disappearance.
Milo's intake medical exam shows no injuries or vocal-cord damage consistent with trauma-induced muteness.
The crayon symbol Milo repeatedly draws is steganographically embedded as a digital watermark inside multiple corrupted security-camera files recovered from separate corners of the village.
Halcyon Vantage holds a decades-old private lease on the restricted military land bordering Amity Hollow.

knows: The residents' meals were abandoned mid-bite with no signs of struggle or forced entry.
All boot prints from the village lead to the ridge trailhead and stop dead at the treeline.
hides: Downplays to the team how much this scene mirrors the case he dismissed before — doesn't want his judgment questioned twice in one career.
believes_falsely: Assumes this is most likely a coordinated mass departure — a cult exodus or staged event — rather than something done to the residents against their will.

knows: Milo's physical exam shows no trauma-consistent injuries.
Milo has drawn the same symbol dozens of times since being found.
hides: Keeps her growing unease about how calm Milo is from the rest of the team, worried that flagging it as 'not textbook' before trust is built will get him pulled into more aggressive questioning.
believes_falsely: Believes Milo's silence is a classic acute dissociative response to witnessed trauma, not a performance he's maintaining on purpose.

knows: The village's smart-home hubs are all running an identical, undocumented firmware version.
hides: Says nothing yet about the odd firmware timestamp, wanting to verify it himself before raising an alarm he can't back up.
believes_falsely: Assumes the corrupted camera files are simple storage failures from a power surge, not something deliberately encoded.

knows: The restricted zone bordering the village is leased to a private corporate tenant, not held directly by the military.
hides: Doesn't volunteer which corporation holds that lease, redirecting the team's questions about the zone toward jurisdiction and paperwork instead.
believes_falsely: Has been told by her contact that this is a contained 'asset protection' matter, and genuinely doesn't yet grasp that the residents were taken against their will.

knows: He was told to keep drawing the symbol and to stay close to whoever runs the investigation.
He knows the firmware update that changed every home in the village didn't come from any resident.
hides: His silence is trained, not traumatic — speaking freely risks ending the task he was left to do.
He was placed in Amity Hollow before the disappearance; he is not a resident's child who simply survived.
believes_falsely: Believes that completing his task well will mean he gets his own family back, though no one who left him here ever actually promised him that.

case.label: Yuna's Double Life
case.solution: Three years ago, crushing medical debt for her mother and a stalled visa case for a family member made Yuna vulnerable to a recruiter with ties to a Halcyon Vantage shell company; in exchange for her mother's debt being quietly paid off, Yuna agreed to feed her handler updates on any investigation that came near the restricted zone. She has convinced herself she's only smoothing bureaucratic friction and protecting her family — she does not yet know the true scale of what she has been enabling.
objective_facts: Yuna's liaison clearance for restricted-zone access was fast-tracked three years ago through a private referral, bypassing the standard vetting queue.
Yuna places an encrypted call to the same unlisted number every night during the investigation, always after the rest of the team has gone quiet.
Yuna's mother's medical debt was paid off in full two years ago by an anonymous benefactor.
The unlisted number Yuna calls traces back to a shell company registered as a Halcyon Vantage subsidiary.

knows: She reports investigation progress nightly to a contact she was introduced to three years ago.
Her mother's medical debt was cleared because of that same contact.
hides: Never mentions the nightly calls to anyone on the team, stepping away from camp each night under the excuse of checking on permits.
believes_falsely: Tells herself this arrangement only ever helped her family and smooths red tape for the government — she has not let herself consider what her reports might actually be used for.

knows: Yuna's zone-access paperwork came through unusually fast for a case this sensitive.
believes_falsely: Chalks Yuna's over-preparedness up to her just being very good at her job, not to any prior relationship with the land they're standing on.

case.label: Wei Zhou's Hidden Grief
case.solution: Wei Zhou's cousin's family — the Zhaos, at 14 Birch Lane — were residents of Amity Hollow and vanished with everyone else. Wei Zhou requested this assignment the moment the case crossed his desk and has told no one on the team that the missing aren't strangers to him; he is running a private, more urgent search alongside the official one.
objective_facts: Wei Zhou personally requested assignment to this case outside the normal staffing rotation.
The Zhao family of 14 Birch Lane, Amity Hollow, are Wei Zhou's cousin's household and are among the forty-one missing.
Wei Zhou carries a personal photo of the Zhao children in his field bag, separate from the case files.

knows: His cousin's family lived at 14 Birch Lane and are among the missing.
He asked to be put on this case the moment he saw the address.
hides: Lets the team believe his intensity on this case is just professional dedication, not because the missing include people he loves.
believes_falsely: Privately assumes that if anyone survived out there, it would be his cousin's family first, since they knew the woods better than most in the village — a hope with nothing behind it yet.

knows: Wei Zhou pushed hard and fast to get assigned to this specific case.
believes_falsely: Reads Wei Zhou's intensity as simple enthusiasm for a rare, high-profile technical case.

episodes.theme: As Mei Lin begins gentle sessions with Milo, the video analysts find his hand-drawn symbol buried as a digital watermark inside corrupted security footage, while Yuna quietly steers the investigation's early questions away from the military land bordering the village.
episodes.intro_note: The symbol survives the render: distorted but unmistakable. As dawn breaks over Amity Hollow, the team turns the discovery into a starting point — and Yuna finds new reasons to keep steering them anywhere but toward the restricted zone.
episodes.outro_note: Yuna slips away from the tent for her nightly call exactly as she has every night before — except tonight, for the first time, Jonah watches her go, and doesn't look away.

knows: Wei Zhou has confirmed the watermark appears in footage from at least three separate camera positions.
The trailhead conceals an old maintenance road leading toward the restricted zone.
hides: Still hasn't told the team how close this feels to the case where he ignored a whistleblower — determined this time to chase every anomaly instead.

knows: Milo's drawn symbol is an exact match for the watermark hidden in the recovered footage.
hides: Still keeps her doubts about Milo's 'textbook' presentation to herself, now sitting on a growing list of small details that don't fit.
believes_falsely: Reads Milo's fixation on drawing the symbol as compulsive re-enactment of trauma, not as him tracking whether the investigation is finding what he was left to show them.

knows: The same watermark is embedded in footage from three different corners of the village, meaning it was planted systematically, not accidentally.
Quietly, he's begun cross-referencing his cousin's address against the recovered footage on his own time.
hides: Still says nothing about his connection to the Zhao family, telling himself he'll speak up only if he finds something concrete.

hides: Watches the team's discovery with quiet, careful interest instead of the fear or confusion an actual traumatized child might show — and works to hide that difference.
believes_falsely: Still believes that if he leads them all the way to the end of the path, his real family will be given back to him.

case.label: The Signal
case.solution: Halcyon Vantage covertly pushed a firmware update to every smart-home device in Amity Hollow that embedded a repeating 17-hertz waveform beneath routine 'sleep sound' audio; sustained exposure induced trance-like suggestibility, and a follow-up broadcast walked all forty-one residents out of their homes and into the woods toward a concealed bunker entrance, where they were taken in as unwitting subjects of an unauthorized behavioral-influence experiment.
objective_facts: All forty-one residents' final recorded device activity falls within an eleven-minute window between 9:52 and 10:03 PM.
A repeating 17-hertz waveform is embedded beneath the ambient 'sleep sound' audio pushed to every smart speaker in the village.
The firmware update carrying the waveform was transmitted six hours before the disappearance from a server registered to a shell subsidiary later traced to Halcyon Vantage.
Boot prints from every household converge at a single point at the Ridge Trailhead and do not double back.

knows: The team has confirmed a deliberate embedded waveform, not equipment malfunction, and the firmware push is now provable.
The boot prints from every household converge at one point on the ridge with no return trail.
hides: He has already drafted an internal report recommending the case be escalated past his own superiors, but hasn't told the team, afraid it invites exactly the shutdown he's trying to avoid.
believes_falsely: He believes the shell subsidiary's registration will take weeks to unravel, not realizing Yuna already knows exactly who controls it.

knows: The waveform is a genuine designed signal, not artifact or noise — the repetition and phase-locking rule out anything natural.
The firmware push originated six hours before the event from a specific external server.
hides: He deliberately underplays how personally rattled the discovery makes him in front of the team, worried it will raise questions about his objectivity.
believes_falsely: He assumes the signal alone explains the residents' compliance, not yet suspecting a second, more targeted broadcast physically walked them into the woods.

knows: The shell subsidiary the firmware server is registered to is one she was already briefed on by her actual handler, though officially she is hearing the name for the first time.
hides: She feigns surprise at the subsidiary's name and steers the team's next step toward slower public-records channels instead of the faster lead she actually has access to.
believes_falsely: She believes she can keep managing what the team learns and when, not yet aware Wei Zhou's technical trail is moving faster than she can redirect it.

knows: The audio forensics summary confirms a designed acoustic component to the disappearance, which reframes every session with Milo as potentially about what he heard, not only what he saw.
hides: She keeps to herself, for now, a growing intuition that Milo's silence might be protective rather than purely traumatic — a choice, not just a wound.
believes_falsely: She believes Milo's silence is a trauma response to what he witnessed, not yet considering that the signal itself — sound — might be why he won't use his voice.

knows: He recognizes the low hum the team keeps playing back — he heard it the night everyone left, though he cannot yet say so.
hides: He hides that he recognizes the sound at all, going still and refusing to look at the speaker whenever Dr. Lin plays it during sessions.
believes_falsely: He believes that if he keeps drawing the symbol and staying silent, the people who told him to wait will come back for him exactly as promised — not realizing 'waiting' was the entire design.

case.label: The Second Employer
case.solution: Yuna Cho was placed on the case through a Halcyon Vantage-linked regional coordination office that operates one level removed from the corporation's name; she reports the investigation's findings and movements to a handler there, genuinely believing she is protecting a legitimate, sanctioned partnership between the government and a defense contractor — not that she is enabling the concealment of an abduction and experiment.
objective_facts: Yuna Cho's liaison appointment to this case was requested by name through a coordination office rather than assigned through standard rotation.
Yuna has placed at least three encrypted calls to an unlisted number since the investigation began, each within an hour of a major discovery being logged.

knows: Her placement on this case was arranged, not random, and her real reporting line runs to a Halcyon-linked coordination office.
hides: She hides every call to her handler behind claims of 'checking permits' or 'chasing paperwork,' timing her most sensitive calls for when the team is occupied elsewhere.
believes_falsely: She believes the arrangement she serves is a legitimate, sanctioned partnership easing friction between agencies — not a mechanism for concealing what happened to Amity Hollow's residents.

knows: Yuna has been unusually smooth and unusually fast at securing things that are normally difficult to get.
hides: He hasn't told the team he's quietly started keeping his own written timeline of every request Yuna has slow-walked, not yet ready to accuse her outright.
believes_falsely: He believes her behavior is careerism — someone managing her own advancement — rather than active, directed concealment.

case.label: The Builder's Guilt
case.solution: Decades ago, Old Chen was the lead systems engineer on a government-contracted disaster-shelter project beneath the ridge; after the contract quietly changed hands to Halcyon Vantage, the facility was sealed off, repurposed, and expanded far beyond its original design. Chen's own granddaughter disappeared nineteen years ago during an early, smaller-scale test of the same influence program later used on Amity Hollow, and he has watched the ridge from his cabin ever since, unable to prove what happened to her or stop what he suspects is still happening.
objective_facts: Old Chen's name appears as lead systems engineer of record on the original 1990s shelter-construction permits for the site beneath the ridge.
Old Chen's granddaughter, Mara Chen, was reported missing from a settlement near the ridge nineteen years ago and was never found.
Old Chen has lived alone in a cabin within sight of the ridge for the past eleven years.
Old Chen possesses hand-drawn blueprints of the original facility that do not match any publicly filed schematics.

knows: He designed the original shelter's ventilation and access systems himself, decades before it was ever called anything like Halcyon Vantage.
hides: He hides that he still keeps a full set of his original blueprints, afraid that handing them over means admitting exactly how much he helped build.
believes_falsely: He has believed for years that whatever happened to his granddaughter was random tragedy, not letting himself connect her disappearance to the facility he built — because if it's connected, it's his fault.

knows: Old Chen clearly knows more about the ridge and what's beneath it than a retiree living off-grid should.
hides: He doesn't tell Chen how close the team already is to tracing the signal toward the ridge, wanting to see if Chen volunteers it first.
believes_falsely: He assumes Chen's evasiveness is just small-town suspicion of outsiders, not a decades-old personal wound.

case.label: The Zhao Family
case.solution: Wei Zhou's cousin's family — the Zhaos, five members including two children — settled in Amity Hollow eight years ago. Wei Zhou has told no one on the team about the connection, both because protocol would likely remove him from an investigation involving his own relatives, and because he isn't ready to face what definitive proof of their fate, rather than lingering uncertainty, might mean.

Case protocol requires analysts to disclose personal connections to any investigation and recuse themselves from directly related evidence.

knows: The Zhao family living in Amity Hollow was his cousin's family, confirmed the moment he recognized a photograph on the neighboring property.
hides: He has said nothing to Jonah or anyone else about the relation, and has quietly pulled the Zhao household's tablet from the evidence queue outside proper hours to search it himself first.
believes_falsely: He believes he can find whatever the tablet holds and process it privately before anyone notices, without realizing his absences have already been noticed.

knows: Wei Zhou has been distracted and has disappeared from the trailer at odd hours since visiting the property next to The Last House.
hides: He hasn't confronted Wei Zhou directly yet, choosing to watch a little longer before deciding whether it's exhaustion or something else.
believes_falsely: He assumes Wei Zhou's odd behavior is ordinary case fatigue, not a connection to a specific missing family.

episodes.theme: The team traces the signal's origin toward the restricted zone's perimeter as Jonah's superiors threaten to shut the investigation down, Yuna is nearly caught relaying details to an outside contact, and Old Chen warns the team off the ridge in a way that reveals more than he intends.
episodes.intro_note: The bearing points into military land, and Old Chen has vanished back up the ridge without answering the one question that matters. Jonah's superiors are already asking why a missing-persons case needs coordinates on restricted ground, and Wei Zhou is still carrying a device he hasn't logged into evidence.
episodes.outro_note: The stand-down order lands in Jonah's inbox with a forty-eight-hour compliance clock attached — but before dawn, Old Chen turns up at the Mobile Forensics Unit with a hand-drawn map of his own making: a maintenance door in the perimeter fence, sealed off every public schematic, and never his to give away. As the team loads into the van, Yuna reaches for her second phone one more time — and this time, Wei Zhou is watching.
sections: Field Ops Tent — The Call From Above: Jonah takes a pressured call from his superiors questioning where this case is headed
Mobile Forensics Unit — Plotting the Bearing: the team triangulates the waveform's origin to a point inside the restricted zone's perimeter (The Signal)
Restricted Zone Checkpoint — A Fence With No Questions: the team is stonewalled at the perimeter by guards who won't confirm or deny anything (The Signal)
Field Ops Tent — Buying Forty-Eight Hours: Jonah negotiates with his chain of command for more time before the case is pulled
The Ridge Trailhead — Off the Ridge: Old Chen intercepts the team and warns them off in terms sharper than folk superstition (The Builder's Guilt)
Old Chen's Cabin — A Name He Shouldn't Know: alone, Chen replays what he just said and realizes he gave away more than he meant to (The Builder's Guilt)
Trauma Safe Room — The Word That Breaks the Crayon: Milo shatters a crayon the instant Mei Lin says 'ridge' in session (The Signal)
Mobile Forensics Unit — Working Late: Jonah notices Wei Zhou's hours have gotten stranger, not better (The Zhao Family)
The Last House — The Voice on the Tablet: Wei Zhou finds a voice memo from his cousin recorded hours before the disappearance (The Zhao Family)
Mobile Forensics Unit — Not Yet: Wei Zhou decides to keep the memo off the case log a little longer (The Zhao Family)
Field Ops Tent — The Second Phone: Yuna takes a call on a device she doesn't use for official business (The Second Employer)
Field Ops Tent — Almost: Jonah walks in mid-call and Yuna covers in the half-second she has (The Second Employer)
Amity Hollow Square — What's Left to Search: the team regroups amid the cold tables, weighing what's left to do before the clock runs out
Old Chen's Cabin — The Map He Shouldn't Draw: Chen decides, against everything he's told himself for years, to help the team off the record (The Builder's Guilt)
Mobile Forensics Unit — A Ping Wei Zhou Can't Explain: Wei Zhou logs an anomalous outbound transmission tied to a device he half-recognizes (The Second Employer)
The Ridge Trailhead — Before Sunrise: the team readies to move on the access point Chen finally gave them (The Signal)
Mobile Forensics Unit — Watching Her Now: as the stand-down clock ticks and the map sits folded on the console, Wei Zhou keeps his eyes on Yuna a beat too long (The Second Employer)

case.label: The Signal
case.solution: Halcyon Vantage covertly pushed a firmware update to every smart-home device in Amity Hollow that embedded a repeating 17-hertz waveform beneath routine 'sleep sound' audio; sustained exposure induced trance-like suggestibility, and a follow-up broadcast walked all forty-one residents out of their homes and into the woods toward a concealed bunker entrance, where they were taken in as unwitting subjects of an unauthorized behavioral-influence experiment.
objective_facts: All forty-one residents' final recorded device activity falls within an eleven-minute window between 9:52 and 10:03 PM.
A repeating 17-hertz waveform is embedded beneath the ambient 'sleep sound' audio pushed to every smart speaker in the village.
The firmware update carrying the waveform was transmitted six hours before the disappearance from a server registered to a shell subsidiary later traced to Halcyon Vantage.
Boot prints from every household converge at a single point at the Ridge Trailhead and do not double back.

knows: Triangulation of the firmware push and the boot-print convergence both resolve to a single origin inside the restricted zone's perimeter.
hides: He hasn't told his superiors how far the triangulation has actually progressed, buying the team more time before an order to stand down becomes unavoidable.
believes_falsely: He believes that once he has hard geographic proof, the shutdown threat will lose its power over him — not yet realizing his superiors already know exactly what's under that ground.

knows: The signal's transmission point resolves to coordinates inside the restricted zone, precise enough to plot on a map.
hides: He keeps the exact coordinates off the shared case log a few hours longer than protocol requires, wanting to check them against something personal first.
believes_falsely: He assumes reaching the coordinates will mean answers about everyone equally, not letting himself consider his own family's fate might be worse than the others'.

knows: Milo's physical reactions are now specific and consistent — the word 'ridge' and a hand-drawn door shape trigger the same shutdown every time.
hides: She hasn't told the team how targeted Milo's reactions have become, wanting more sessions before turning his responses into investigative data he never agreed to give.
believes_falsely: She still frames his shutdown as protective avoidance of memory, not yet entertaining that he might be following an instruction rather than avoiding a trauma.

knows: The word 'ridge' means exactly what he's afraid it means, and he knows precisely what's out there because he was told.
hides: He hides his reaction by immediately switching to drawing the same door-shaped mark instead of answering, redirecting Dr. Lin's attention the way he's learned works.
believes_falsely: He continues to believe his silence and his drawings are fulfilling his end of a bargain that will bring the people back — not recognizing that no one who gave him that instruction ever intended to keep it.

case.label: The Second Employer
case.solution: Yuna Cho was placed on the case through a Halcyon Vantage-linked regional coordination office that operates one level removed from the corporation's name; she reports the investigation's findings and movements to a handler there, genuinely believing she is protecting a legitimate, sanctioned partnership between the government and a defense contractor — not that she is enabling the concealment of an abduction and experiment.
objective_facts: Yuna Cho's liaison appointment to this case was requested by name through a coordination office rather than assigned through standard rotation.
Yuna has placed at least three encrypted calls to an unlisted number since the investigation began, each within an hour of a major discovery being logged.
The unlisted number Yuna calls routes through the same regional exchange as Halcyon Vantage's corporate offices.
Yuna has never once flagged or escalated any finding pointing toward the restricted zone, despite her official role requiring exactly that.

knows: The team has triangulated the signal's origin to within the restricted zone's perimeter, information she hasn't reported yet — she wants to see whether the investigation gets killed before she has to.
hides: She takes an outside call on a second phone kept hidden in the tent's supply crate, and nearly leaves it in the open when Jonah walks in.
believes_falsely: She still believes her handler's assurances that this is 'containment,' not disappearance — a belief starting to crack under the specificity of what the team keeps finding.

knows: He walks in on Yuna hurriedly ending a call on a phone that isn't the one she uses for official business.
hides: He says nothing about the phone in the moment, deciding to watch rather than confront, and doesn't tell the rest of the team what he saw.
believes_falsely: He still isn't certain whether what he saw was evidence of betrayal or just Yuna handling something personal, and doesn't yet suspect the true scale of who she answers to.

knows: He notices an anomalous outbound data ping logged at an hour when the only active device was the one Yuna claims not to use in the field.
hides: He flags it only in a private note file for now, not yet sure it means what he thinks it means.
believes_falsely: He assumes, for now, it's more likely a diagnostic artifact from the mesh network than anything deliberate.

Old Chen possesses hand-drawn blueprints of the original facility that do not match any publicly filed schematics.

knows: He knows the exact maintenance access point on the ridge that isn't on any public map, because he built it himself.
hides: He warns the team off the ridge in terms specific enough — naming a ventilation stack by an engineer's term — that he immediately regrets it, then tries to walk the warning back as folk superstition.
believes_falsely: He tells himself warning them off is enough, that he doesn't have to decide yet whether to actually help them in — a delay he knows, underneath, he can't sustain much longer.

knows: Chen's warning included a structural term no local rumor would produce — someone who built the place talks like that.
hides: He doesn't press Chen on the phrase in the moment, letting him retreat, calculating that pushing too hard now will close him off for good.
believes_falsely: He assumes he has time to come back and work on Chen slowly, not sensing how close Chen already is to breaking on his own.

Case protocol requires analysts to disclose personal connections to any investigation and recuse themselves from directly related evidence.

knows: The tablet he pulled from evidence holds a partial voice memo from his cousin, recorded hours before the disappearance, saying the kids 'won't stop looking at the speaker.'
hides: He hasn't logged the voice memo into the case file yet, telling himself he needs to verify its authenticity first, when really he isn't ready to make it evidence instead of the last thing he has of her voice.
believes_falsely: He believes keeping the memo private a little longer causes no harm to the investigation, not recognizing it's the clearest firsthand account yet of the signal's effect on a listener.

knows: He directly asks Wei Zhou where he keeps disappearing to and gets a deflection instead of an answer.
hides: He decides not to escalate the question to a formal one yet, choosing loyalty to a trusted colleague over procedure — a choice he doesn't examine too closely.
believes_falsely: He still assumes whatever Wei Zhou is hiding is personal and unrelated to the case itself, rather than case evidence he's sitting on.

episodes.theme: A breach at the restricted zone's checkpoint uncovers evidence tying the land directly to the corporation Halcyon Vantage, upending the team's trust in Yuna and in each other, and ending on the discovery of a hidden entrance leading underground.
episodes.intro_note: Jonah's superiors ordered the investigation stood down at dusk, but Old Chen's warning — that some doors, once you find them, don't let you walk away clean — convinced Jonah they were closer than ever. Under cover of night, and against direct orders, the team moves on the checkpoint.
episodes.outro_note: The hatch is real, cut into the ridge exactly where the drone map pointed, and Old Chen's silence as he stares down into it says more than any warning he's given so far. Whatever the team has been chasing since Amity Hollow's dinner tables went cold, it isn't behind a door anymore — it's beneath their feet, and someone on this team already knew it was there.
sections: Narrator sets the scene: night at Field Ops, the team gathered despite an order to stand down
Jonah rallies the team to move on the checkpoint before Old Chen's warning goes cold
Wei Zhou reveals he's cracked the checkpoint's private badge network
Yuna pushes back hard against the plan, her smooth reasoning cracking at the edges
Old Chen offers to guide them down a service road he built decades ago
In the Trauma Safe Room, Milo grows agitated at the word 'checkpoint' and won't say why
The team moves through the dark toward the checkpoint on Old Chen's old road
At the checkpoint, Wei Zhou kills the cameras and Jonah forces the gate
Inside the guard office, they find a badge system registered to Meridian Sentry Services
Jonah uncovers a supply manifest listing the exact smart-home hubs found in every Amity Hollow home
Yuna is caught trying to slip the manifest into her own bag
Jonah confronts Yuna in a low, furious voice, and she deflects without quite denying it
Wei Zhou quietly matches a hub serial number to his cousin Lian's household
Back at the Mobile Forensics Unit, thermal drone imaging shows ventilation venting from land marked undeveloped on every map
Old Chen studies the drone map and goes very still, recognizing exactly where it leads
Following the vent site, the team finds a concealed hatch cut into the ridge itself
Narrator closes on the hatch standing open in the dark, and on Old Chen's face, which already knows what's waiting underneath

case.label: Yuna's Double Life
case.solution: Yuna Cho has spent the entire investigation secretly briefing a Halcyon Vantage contact on the team's progress in exchange for a promised corporate position and quiet handling of her parents' stalled immigration paperwork; she tells herself she is only 'managing information flow,' not realizing how directly her reports have let the corporation stay one step ahead of the team at every turn.
objective_facts: Yuna Cho's personal phone shows a recurring pattern of short encrypted calls to an unregistered number immediately after every investigation briefing.
The liaison assignment placing Yuna on this case originated from a direct request in a memo from Halcyon Vantage's regional counsel, not the standard government rotation.
Yuna filed the paperwork in the investigation's first week that quietly narrowed the search perimeter to exclude the checkpoint's east gate.
Halcyon Vantage's human resources system shows a conditional job offer extended to Yuna Cho, dated before the investigation began.

knows: That the badge and guard-rotation system at the checkpoint belongs to Meridian Sentry Services, a Halcyon Vantage subsidiary, not the Department of Defense.
That her contact ordered her, weeks ago, to keep the team away from the checkpoint's east gate for as long as possible.
hides: That she just tried to slip the supply manifest listing the smart-home hub shipments into her own bag before Jonah saw it, because it would directly tie Halcyon Vantage to Amity Hollow's homes.
That she has been relaying a summary of the team's findings to her contact after nearly every major discovery.
believes_falsely: That she can keep steering the investigation without anyone actually getting hurt, and that this makes what she's doing different from betrayal.

knows: That he personally caught Yuna attempting to conceal the supply manifest at the checkpoint.
hides: That he hasn't told the rest of the team yet what he saw, because he wants proof of how deep it goes before he moves against her.
believes_falsely: That Yuna's panic in the moment was about protecting her career, not about protecting whoever she's really working for.

case.label: Old Chen's Buried Guilt
case.solution: Old Chen was the lead systems engineer on the original 1988 shelter facility beneath the ridge; when the site was quietly transferred to Halcyon Vantage under a research contract years later, his granddaughter Iris volunteered for what she believed was a paid wellness study and never came home. Old Chen has lived off-grid near the ridge ever since, monitoring radio traffic and telling no one, too ashamed of the part he played in building the place to bring his suspicions to any authority.
objective_facts: Public utility records list Old Chen as the systems engineer of record on the facility's original 1988 blueprints.
Iris Chen was reported missing eleven years ago from a town forty miles from Amity Hollow; the case was closed as a runaway within a month.
Old Chen's cabin radio logs contain years of intercepted low-frequency bursts matching the exact signal signature recovered from Amity Hollow's smart-home devices.
Halcyon Vantage's acquisition records show the facility changed hands from a decommissioned government contract fourteen years ago — three years before Iris disappeared.

knows: That the service road he's guiding the team down was one he designed himself decades ago, back when the facility was still government-run.
That the low-frequency signal signature matches transmissions he's been quietly logging on his own radio for years.
hides: That he has never told anyone, including the authorities, that his granddaughter Iris went missing after answering an ad for a 'wellness study' he now believes was connected to this same facility.
believes_falsely: That if he can just get the investigators close enough without saying her name out loud, he can find out what happened to Iris without having to admit his own part in building the place that took her.

knows: That Old Chen clearly recognizes far more about the facility's layout than an old blueprint alone would explain.
believes_falsely: That Old Chen's knowledge is purely professional, from his engineering background, rather than personal.

case.label: Wei Zhou's Hidden Stake
case.solution: Wei Zhou's cousin Lian and her family lived in Amity Hollow under her married surname, Zhang, and vanished with everyone else; Wei Zhou recognized her voice in the household's smart-speaker logs within the investigation's first days but said nothing, afraid that disclosing the connection would get him pulled from the one case that might find her.
objective_facts: The Zhang household's smart-speaker logs contain a female voice that matches, in a private waveform comparison Wei Zhou ran off-book, an old family recording of his cousin Lian.
Wei Zhou submitted two separate requests for unsupervised access to raw Zhang-residence audio evidence, both denied through normal channels before this batch.
Wei Zhou's official conflict-of-interest disclosure lists no personal connection to any Amity Hollow resident.

knows: That a hub serial number recovered from the checkpoint's supply manifest matches, exactly, the model installed in his cousin Lian's home under her married name, Zhang.
hides: That he has known since the investigation's first week that his cousin's family is among the vanished, and has said nothing to avoid being removed from the case.
believes_falsely: That he can keep working the case dispassionately, right up until the moment he sees proof in front of him instead of a name on a list.

case.label: The Halcyon Vantage Cover-Up
case.solution: Halcyon Vantage quietly acquired the decommissioned government shelter years ago through a shell research contract and has been running an unauthorized behavioral-conditioning program using targeted low-frequency audio broadcast through consumer smart-home devices; Amity Hollow was a live-scale field test of the technology, and the checkpoint's guards are contracted through a Halcyon Vantage subsidiary, Meridian Sentry Services, rather than the military, which is why the paper trail never matched.
objective_facts: The checkpoint's guard rotation logs run through a private contractor badge system registered to Meridian Sentry Services, a Halcyon Vantage subsidiary, not the Department of Defense.
A supply manifest recovered from a breached checkpoint locker lists bulk shipments of the exact smart-home hub model found in every Amity Hollow home.
Thermal imaging from a drone pass shows active ventilation exhaust venting from a site marked as undeveloped land on every public zoning map.
Halcyon Vantage's public research filings make no mention of any behavioral or acoustic research program, despite internal shipment records tying its subsidiary to the hardware used in Amity Hollow.

knows: That the checkpoint's badge system and supply manifest both trace back to Meridian Sentry Services, a Halcyon Vantage subsidiary.
believes_falsely: That finding this paper trail means the worst of the danger is behind them, rather than just beginning.

knows: That the thermal imaging shows ventilation venting from land the public zoning maps list as undeveloped, meaning something large is built directly beneath it.
believes_falsely: That the vent site marks the facility's full extent, not realizing it's only one small exhaust point of something much larger.

knows: That Meridian Sentry Services is a Halcyon Vantage shell used to staff the checkpoint without triggering federal oversight.
hides: That she has known this since before the investigation began, and that her 'liaison' role exists specifically to manage what the team is allowed to find here.
believes_falsely: That the team still sees her only as slow and overly cautious, not as someone actively working against them.

episodes.theme: Inside the bunker network the team finds row after row of sealed observation chambers and proof this was no rescue but an experiment, while Old Chen learns the fate of the granddaughter he lost to the very facility he once helped build, and Yuna leads the team deeper under the guise of helping them.
episodes.intro_note: The hatch Old Chen already dreaded leads down into a stairwell cut straight into the mountain. Whatever waited beneath the ridge, Old Chen's face made clear he never expected to be the one leading them to it.
episodes.outro_note: Old Chen has said Iris's name out loud for the first time in eleven years, and Wei Zhou has finally said Lian's — but neither confession is what stops the team cold. It's the corridor lights, shifting one by one from white to red as they reach the control room door, and the sound, distant but unmistakable, of something enormous beginning to seal itself shut around them.

First full view of the atrium, dormant screens and an impossible scale of concrete

Wei Zhou finds a corridor lined with sealed observation chambers
The team realizes the chambers were built for watching, not sheltering
A chamber plaque lists an Amity Hollow resident's name and a status code no one can explain
Wei Zhou finds a chamber logged under the name Zhang and goes silent
Wei Zhou finally tells Jonah the truth about his cousin Lian
In the Trauma Safe Room above, Milo draws the bunker's corridor layout in exact detail he was never shown
Old Chen, patched in by radio, recognizes the chamber numbering as his own decades-old design
Old Chen insists on coming down himself, over Jonah's objections

Old Chen finally tells Jonah the truth about his granddaughter and what he helped build
Yuna receives a covert instruction through her earpiece and hides it from the others

That she has just received a coded instruction through her earpiece to keep the team moving toward the central control corridor.
hides: That she recognizes exactly what kind of facility this is and has chosen to keep guiding the team deeper anyway.

believes_falsely: That this is the last hidden connection left on the team, not realizing Yuna's is far deeper.

case.label: The Halcyon Vantage Cover-Up
case.solution: Halcyon Vantage quietly acquired the decommissioned government shelter years ago through a shell research contract and has been running an unauthorized behavioral-conditioning program using targeted low-frequency audio broadcast through consumer smart-home devices; Amity Hollow was a live-scale field test of the technology, and the checkpoint's guards are contracted through a Halcyon Vantage subsidiary, Meridian Sentry Services, rather than the military, which is why the paper trail never matched.
objective_facts: The checkpoint's guard rotation logs run through a private contractor badge system registered to Meridian Sentry Services, a Halcyon Vantage subsidiary, not the Department of Defense.
A supply manifest recovered from a breached checkpoint locker lists bulk shipments of the exact smart-home hub model found in every Amity Hollow home.
Thermal imaging from a drone pass shows active ventilation exhaust venting from a site marked as undeveloped land on every public zoning map.
Halcyon Vantage's public research filings make no mention of any behavioral or acoustic research program, despite internal shipment records tying its subsidiary to the hardware used in Amity Hollow.

knows: That the sealed chambers below prove this was never a shelter — it was built, or repurposed, to observe people, not protect them.
believes_falsely: That the team still has the element of surprise, not realizing Yuna's contact already knows exactly where they are.

knows: That the chamber logs use structured subject IDs consistent with a long-running experimental protocol, not an emergency shelter roster.
believes_falsely: That the facility's purpose ended with Amity Hollow, not realizing the chamber logs show years of prior subjects predating the village entirely.

knows: That the corridor she's guiding the team toward houses the facility's active control room, not an exit.
hides: That she was told this morning that the situation has moved from 'contain the investigation's information' to 'contain the investigators.'
believes_falsely: That she'll be able to step in before anything happens to the team, rather than simply being the one who led them into position.

episodes.theme: Halcyon Vantage's security locks the team inside the bunker the moment they get too close to the truth, forcing a confrontation that finally exposes the full extent of Yuna's betrayal and pushes Old Chen toward one last act to make up for what he built.
episodes.intro_note: Yuna led Jonah and Wei Zhou through the hidden entrance under the guise of helping them, and the last thing anyone above ground heard before the connection dropped was the sound of heavy doors closing somewhere far below.
episodes.outro_note: Halcyon's convoy reaches the ridge just as Old Chen reaches the shaft door only he remembers how to open — and the last word out of his radio, cut off mid-sentence, is Jonah's name.
sections: Emergency blast doors seal the bunker atrium without warning, trapping Jonah, Wei Zhou, and Yuna inside (The Halcyon Experiment)
Jonah and Wei Zhou search for a manual override while Yuna insists she'll 'handle it' through her contacts (The Halcyon Experiment / Yuna's Double Life)
The observation deck's monitors flicker to life, showing all forty-one chambers occupied and vitals steady (The Halcyon Experiment)
Wei Zhou recognizes his cousin's family in Chamber 19 and finally breaks his silence about why this case was never just a job (The Halcyon Experiment)
Jonah presses Yuna on a discrepancy in the checkpoint's radio logs from the night of the breach (Yuna's Double Life)
Old Chen, listening on his old scanner, hears the team go dark and remembers the maintenance shaft he built and sealed decades ago (Old Chen's Buried Guilt)
In the safe room above ground, Milo redraws his symbol with a new mark at its center, and Mei Lin can't tell if it's progress or panic (Milo's True Role)
Field Ops loses contact with the underground team and debates whether to formally notify Halcyon Vantage of the breach (The Halcyon Experiment)
Jonah finds a second device sewn into Yuna's jacket lining, and her composure finally cracks (Yuna's Double Life)
Wei Zhou matches an engineering stamp on the bunker's support beams to a name he's seen before: Chen (Old Chen's Buried Guilt)
An unmarked Halcyon security convoy rolls up to the checkpoint and turns off-road, straight toward the ridge (The Halcyon Experiment)
Old Chen loads a canvas bag with forty-year-old keys and codes and starts walking toward the ridge alone, leaving a note he hopes no one reads too soon (Old Chen's Buried Guilt)
Cornered, Yuna admits Halcyon calls the missing residents a 'trial' and that Amity Hollow was never meant to be the only one (The Halcyon Experiment / Yuna's Double Life)
Old Chen reaches the trailhead as headlights sweep the treeline behind him (Old Chen's Buried Guilt)
A new automated protocol begins sealing the observation deck itself, and the air in the bunker starts to thin (The Halcyon Experiment)
Old Chen's radio goes to static mid-sentence, and Jonah realizes the only way out was just cut off (Old Chen's Buried Guilt / The Halcyon Experiment)

case.label: The Halcyon Experiment
case.solution: Halcyon Vantage, a private behavioral-research contractor, chose Amity Hollow as an isolated test site for a program its internal files call the Continuity Trial. Three years ago the corporation funded free 'SmartHearth' home devices for the village through a shell-company grant, embedding infrasonic transmitters inside them. On the night of the disappearance, Halcyon technicians remotely broadcast an escalating low-frequency signal through every device at once, inducing a trance-like suggestibility in anyone within range; a second, simple audio cue then walked all forty-one residents out of their homes and down a pre-mapped path into the woods and through a hidden entrance into a Cold-War-era bunker beneath the restricted zone, originally engineered decades earlier by Old Chen for a different purpose entirely. The residents are alive, sedated, and monitored in sealed observation chambers as ongoing test subjects — Amity Hollow was never meant to be the only village Halcyon ran the Continuity Trial on.
objective_facts: The SmartHearth devices given to Amity Hollow three years ago were funded by a rural-modernization grant traced back to a shell company controlled by Halcyon Vantage.
Outgoing device logs show three escalating infrasonic pulses broadcast over eleven minutes, ending at the exact minute of the last resident phone ping.
The bunker beneath the restricted zone contains forty-one sealed, currently occupied observation chambers — matching Amity Hollow's population exactly.
The bunker's original structural blueprints carry an engineering stamp matching Old Chen's licensed name from decades before Halcyon Vantage existed.
Internal Halcyon documents recovered from the facility refer to the operation by the code name 'Continuity Trial.'

knows: The observation deck's monitors show all forty-one residents alive and sedated in sealed chambers.
Halcyon Vantage security sealed the bunker's exits the moment the team got close to the chambers.
hides: He says nothing yet to Wei Zhou about suspecting Yuna's radio contact runs outside the government chain, wanting proof before he risks the team turning on each other.
believes_falsely: He assumes this lockdown is a local security overreaction rather than evidence of a program built to be repeated elsewhere.

knows: He's identified his cousin's family alive in Chamber 19.
The bunker's support structure was engineered decades before Halcyon Vantage existed.
hides: He still hasn't told Jonah that this case is personal — that his own family is behind the glass — afraid it will get him pulled off the investigation.
believes_falsely: He assumes finding his cousin's family alive means a straightforward rescue, not grasping yet that Halcyon considers them ongoing test subjects.

knows: The full scope of the Continuity Trial and that Amity Hollow was a pilot for a program meant to be repeated.
hides: She refuses to name her handler even after Jonah finds her second device, protecting the one relationship she thinks might still get her out of this.
believes_falsely: She still believes Halcyon Vantage will extract her quietly if she stays useful, not realizing how disposable she's already become to them.

knows: His own blueprint stamp is on the bunker's original structure, and that the facility has been repurposed from what he built it for.
hides: He still hasn't told the team the full story of his granddaughter, ashamed of how long he stayed silent about the ridge.
believes_falsely: He believes going in alone will spare the team the risk, not recognizing that he needs their help as much as they now need his.

case.label: Yuna's Double Life
case.solution: Yuna Cho was recruited by Halcyon Vantage two years before this assignment and placed as the government liaison specifically to manage and contain any investigation into the restricted zone. Her real job has been to relay the forensics team's progress to a Halcyon handler in real time and to steer, stall, or misdirect them whenever they got close to the bunker — all while maintaining a convincing cover as a helpful facilitator with nothing to hide.
objective_facts: Yuna carries a second, encrypted phone whose only contact routes through a private Halcyon Vantage relay number.
Her personnel file lists an unexplained six-week 'training leave' at a Halcyon Vantage regional facility two years before this posting.
Checkpoint radio logs show Yuna forwarded Jonah's clearance requests to an outside number before submitting them through her official government channel.

knows: She has been reporting the investigation's progress to a Halcyon Vantage handler since before the team arrived at Amity Hollow.
hides: She lies to Jonah's face about the discrepancy in the checkpoint radio logs until the second device is found on her.
believes_falsely: She believes her usefulness to Halcyon still buys her protection, unaware her handler stopped answering her messages an hour ago.

knows: He's found a second, hidden device sewn into Yuna's jacket lining.
hides: He doesn't yet tell Wei Zhou how long he's suspected her, embarrassed he let it go this far without acting.
believes_falsely: He assumed her polish and fast answers were just competence, not a rehearsed cover.

knows: He witnesses Yuna's composure break the moment the second device is found.
believes_falsely: He assumed Yuna's help getting his clearance approved early was a favor, not realizing she was clearing a path she needed open for her own reporting.

case.label: Old Chen's Buried Guilt
case.solution: Decades ago, Old Chen was the lead engineer who designed and built the underground facility now beneath the restricted zone, believing it was a legitimate government shelter project. Years later, a corporate predecessor to Halcyon Vantage ran an early, smaller version of the same signal experiment in a neighboring settlement — and Old Chen's own granddaughter, Grace, was among those taken. His family was told she had relocated and quietly paid to stop asking questions. Old Chen has spent every year since living near the ridge, listening for any sign the facility was active again, unable to prove anything until Amity Hollow gave him one.
objective_facts: Old Chen's engineering stamp appears on the bunker's original 1980s-era blueprints.
Company records show a girl named Grace Chen was reported 'relocated with family' by a Halcyon Vantage predecessor company twenty-two years ago and was never traced afterward.
Old Chen kept decades of handwritten logs tracking unexplained radio and power activity from the ridge.

knows: He built the original shaft and still remembers where its old maintenance door is hidden.
hides: He leaves without telling Field Ops where he's going, writing a note instead of asking permission he's afraid they'd refuse.
believes_falsely: He believes he can reach the shaft and open it before Halcyon's security realizes anyone is coming from outside.

knows: The stamp on the support beams matches a name — Chen — that he's now certain isn't a coincidence.
believes_falsely: He assumes whoever stamped these beams was a Halcyon employee, not an outside engineer who built it for someone else entirely.

knows: Field Ops has lost radio contact with an outside source that was tracking the team's location before the lockdown.
believes_falsely: He assumes any rescue will have to come through official channels, not realizing Old Chen is already walking to the ridge alone.

case.label: Milo's True Role
case.solution: Milo was never a random surviving child. Raised from early childhood in a foster arrangement quietly funded by a Halcyon Vantage affiliate, he was conditioned to present as a mute trauma survivor and fitted with a small subdermal device that made him immune to the Continuity Trial's signal. He was deliberately left behind in Amity Hollow to draw investigators toward the bunker — his recurring symbol a coded map fragment, not a trauma expression — ensuring the corporation could also study how outside authorities could be guided, and misled, into finding what Halcyon wanted them to find.
objective_facts: A late medical scan shows a small subdermal implant behind Milo's left ear that matches no known medical device.
The symbol Milo repeatedly draws is identical to a schematic marker used on Halcyon Vantage's internal facility maps.
Milo has no traceable birth or school record predating his foster placement eighteen months before the Amity Hollow assignment.

knows: The symbol he draws is a map, and he knows exactly which detail to add now that the team is close to the chambers.
hides: He hides that the new mark at the center of his drawing is a deliberate cue, not an escalation of fear.
believes_falsely: He believes finishing what he was placed to do will finally let him go home, a promise made to him long before Amity Hollow.

knows: Milo has added a new, more detailed mark to the center of his recurring symbol.
hides: She doesn't mention the new detail to Jonah yet, wanting to understand it herself first so she doesn't frighten Milo with more questions.
believes_falsely: She reads his agitation as a trauma response nearing a breakthrough, not as a sign he is completing a task.

episodes.theme: In a desperate escape the team hijacks a satellite uplink to broadcast the raw footage of Amity Hollow's disappearance to the world, and in the final recovered frame Milo's true role as Halcyon Vantage's inside agent is revealed, exposing that the investigators didn't find the truth: they were led to it.
episodes.intro_note: The radio went to static just as Old Chen reached the door only he remembered how to open, and for one unbearable stretch, no one below or above the ridge knew if he'd made it through.
sections: Old Chen forces the rusted shaft door open seconds ahead of the security patrol (Old Chen's Buried Guilt)
Static resolves into Old Chen's voice on the emergency channel, guiding the trapped team toward the shaft from memory (Old Chen's Buried Guilt / The Halcyon Experiment)
On the observation deck, Yuna has to choose between her exit plan and helping the team reach the shaft in time (Yuna's Double Life)
Wei Zhou refuses to leave without trying to wake the residents, and Jonah has to make him understand there's no time (The Halcyon Experiment)
The team confirms the chambers are on a separate life-support loop that will hold before proper help can be sent back (The Halcyon Experiment)
Jonah, Wei Zhou, and Yuna climb out through the shaft into open air for the first time in what feels like days (Old Chen's Buried Guilt)
Guards fan out along the checkpoint fence line, and the team realizes they can't simply walk back the way they came (The Halcyon Experiment)
Wei Zhou spots the hijacked satellite uplink van used earlier by field techs, still keyed and idling at the tree line (The Halcyon Experiment)
Inside the van, Wei Zhou wires the corrupted footage — Milo's symbol, the signal, the chambers — into a single raw feed while Jonah holds the door (The Halcyon Experiment)
In the Halcyon Vantage lobby, executives realize the uplink is live and scramble to have it killed before it reaches anyone (The Halcyon Experiment)
Yuna grabs the van's second handset — for one breath it's unclear whether she's calling her handlers or calling it off (Yuna's Double Life)
The feed clears the last relay and goes out to every open channel in range (The Halcyon Experiment)

At Field Ops, the tent erupts as news feeds start running Amity Hollow's own footage back at the world, and Old Chen finally says aloud what he built beneath that ridge — and who he lost to it (Old Chen's Buried Guilt / The Halcyon Experiment)
Federal agents arrive to take Yuna Cho into custody before she can reach any vehicle at all (Yuna's Double Life)
In the safe room, Mei Lin lets Milo watch the broadcast with her, certain the worst is finally over for him (Milo's True Role)
Reviewing the recovered footage one last time for the record, a technician freezes on a single frame from the night of the disappearance (Milo's True Role)
In that frame, half a second long, ten-year-old Milo turns from the fleeing crowd to give a small, deliberate signal to a camera no one else ever noticed was there (Milo's True Role)

knows: The full shape of the Continuity Trial once the raw footage is assembled end to end — the signal, the march into the woods, the chambers.
Wei Zhou's family connection to the case, finally said aloud in the shaft.
His cousin's family is alive but can't be safely woken until proper medical teams arrive on a separate life-support loop.
The world now has the same raw footage he pieced together.
Halcyon Vantage has already gone silent on her, and that helping the team reach the shaft is the only leverage she has left.
hides: She still doesn't name every handler she's worked for, even in custody, unwilling to give up everything at once.
believes_falsely: She hoped cooperating at the end might soften what happens to her next; the agents waiting at the checkpoint make clear it won't.

knows: The broadcast is the only way to make sure what happened to Grace, and to Amity Hollow, can never quietly disappear again.

knows: The broadcast will end her usefulness to Halcyon and expose everything she's done.
hides: In her last free minutes, she still won't say her handler's name out loud, protecting some final piece of leverage.
believes_falsely: She believed until the last second she might slip away in the chaos of the broadcast; the agents at the checkpoint prove otherwise.

knows: The full extent of what Yuna did, confirmed by the second device and her own admissions underground.
Yuna's help reaching the shaft doesn't undo what she did, and he says as much to her before the agents arrive.

knows: He got the team out through the same shaft he swore he'd sealed forever, and finally says at Field Ops what he built beneath that ridge and who he lost to it.
Old Chen built the facility decades ago and lost his granddaughter to an earlier version of the same experiment, finally said aloud at Field Ops.
Why Old Chen carried this alone for so long, once he hears him say it at Field Ops.

knows: His part in this is finished the moment the broadcast goes out, and that no one watching beside him has any idea.
hides: He gives no sign to Mei Lin that the footage playing on the screen means anything different to him than it does to her.

knows: Milo watches the broadcast beside her without reacting, which she reads as relief.
